---
title: "Two-dimensional jittering with ggscatie"
format: html
---

**Abstract** 
For various reasons it is useful for spreading points in dotplot or scatterplot displays. When points are overplotted in a display the assessment of the distribution or association between variables may not be accurately interpreted. There is a long history of using dotplots for examining distributions, with a variety of recommended methods for spreading apart identical points. 


In this work, we introduce methods for adding controlled random noise in two dimensions (i.e. jittering), making it easier to visualize overlapping data points in scatterplots where both x and y variables may have discrete or semi-discrete values. The methods are implemented in `ggscatie` R package that extends `geom_jitter()` on two dimensions, which is accecible in this repository: https://github.com/natydasilva/ggscatie and it will be available on CRAN. 


**Authors**: 
Natalia da Silva, Ignacio Alvarez-Castro, Dianne Cook & Jayani P., Gamage.

**github**: 
https://github.com/natydasilva/jitter2D
